perturbation.original_text: The Fund hereby appoints [●], and [●] hereby accepts such appointment, as the exclusive Remarketing Agent of the VRRM-MFP Shares for the Variable Rate Remarketed Mode for the purpose of establishing on each Business Day the Dividend Rate in respect of the VRRM-MFP Shares and, in connection with a tender, remarketing such VRRM-MFP Shares on behalf of the Beneficial Owners or Holders thereof, as applicable, and calculating the Purchase Price therefor, among other things; and performing such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agent in the Supplement, all pursuant to the procedures set forth in the Supplement and this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Fund hereby appoints [●], and [●] hereby accepts such appointment, as the exclusive Remarketing Agent of the VRRM-MFP Shares for the Variable Rate Remarketed Mode for the purpose of establishing on each Business Day the Dividend Rate in respect of the VRRM-MFP Shares and, in connection with a tender, remarketing such VRRM-MFP Shares on behalf of the Beneficial Owners or Holders thereof, as applicable, and calculating the Purchase Price therefor, among other things; and performing such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agent in the Supplement, all pursuant to the procedures set forth in the Supplement and this Agreement. However, the Fund reserves the right to appoint additional remarketing agents at its discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that the Remarketing Agent is the 'exclusive' agent. Adding the phrase 'However, the Fund reserves the right to appoint additional remarketing agents at its discretion' directly contradicts the exclusivity clause, creating uncertainty about the Remarketing Agent's role and potentially leading to disputes. The contradiction arises because one part of the agreement establishes an exclusive relationship, while another part allows for multiple agents, creating ambiguity about the scope of the Remarketing Agent's responsibilities and rights. This insertion introduces an In-Text contradiction to Section 2(a) Appointment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Appointment. The Fund hereby appoints [●], and [●] hereby accepts such appointment, as the exclusive Remarketing Agent of the VRRM-MFP Shares for the Variable Rate Remarketed Mode for the purpose of establishing on each Business Day the Dividend Rate in respect of the VRRM-MFP Shares and, in connection with a tender, remarketing such VRRM-MFP Shares on behalf of the Beneficial Owners or Holders thereof, as applicable, and calculating the Purchase Price therefor, among other things; and performing such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agent in the Supplement, all pursuant to the procedures set forth in the Supplement and this Agreement. However, the Fund reserves the right to appoint additional remarketing agents at its discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text and the contradicted text both state that the Remarketing Agent is the 'exclusive' agent. The changed text then adds 'However, the Fund reserves the right to appoint additional remarketing agents at its discretion'. This directly clashes with the concept of exclusivity, as the fund now has the right to appoint additional agents, breaking the exclusivity provision. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For the performance of its services as Remarketing Agent hereunder, the Fund shall pay to the Remarketing Agent in arrears on the first day of each calendar month (or, if such day is not a Business Day, on the next succeeding Business Day) a monthly fee for each MFP Share Outstanding on the first calendar day of the preceding calendar month (or the date hereof in the case of the first such payment), in an amount, rounded upward to the nearest dollar, equal to (a) the product of (i) the rate of compensation as then in effect, as shall be agreed upon from time to time in writing by the Fund and the Remarketing Agent, times $[●] multiplied by (ii) the actual number of days from and including such first calendar day of the preceding calendar month (or the date hereof in the case of the first such payment) to and including the last calendar day of such preceding calendar month or, if applicable, the date of termination of this Agreement, if earlier, or the date of any prior redemption or liquidation for such share (as the case may be), divided by (b) 360.
perturbation.changed_text: For the performance of its services as Remarketing Agent hereunder, the Fund shall pay to the Remarketing Agent in arrears on the first day of each calendar month (or, if such day is not a Business Day, on the next succeeding Business Day) a monthly fee for each MFP Share Outstanding on the first calendar day of the preceding calendar month (or the date hereof in the case of the first such payment), in an amount, rounded upward to the nearest dollar, equal to (a) the product of (i) the rate of compensation as then in effect, as shall be agreed upon from time to time in writing by the Fund and the Remarketing Agent, times $[●] multiplied by (ii) the actual number of days from and including such first calendar day of the preceding calendar month (or the date hereof in the case of the first such payment) to and including the last calendar day of such preceding calendar month or, if applicable, the date of termination of this Agreement, if earlier, or the date of any prior redemption or liquidation for such share (as the case may be), divided by (b) 360. The Fund reserves the right to unilaterally adjust the rate of compensation with 30 days' notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies a detailed calculation for the Remarketing Agent's fees, implying a fixed agreement. Adding 'The Fund reserves the right to unilaterally adjust the rate of compensation with 30 days' notice' creates a contradiction. This statement introduces an in-text contradiction because the original fee structure implies a mutually agreed-upon and stable compensation, while the added phrase allows the Fund to change the rate unilaterally, introducing ambiguity and potential disputes over compensation. This introduces an In-Text contradiction to Section 4. Fees and Expenses
perturbation.contradicted_text: Fees and Expenses. For the performance of its services as Remarketing Agent hereunder, the Fund shall pay to the Remarketing Agent in arrears on the first day of each calendar month (or, if such day is not a Business Day, on the next succeeding Business Day) a monthly fee for each MFP Share Outstanding on the first calendar day of the preceding calendar month (or the date hereof in the case of the first such payment), in an amount, rounded upward to the nearest dollar, equal to (a) the product of (i) the rate of compensation as then in effect, as shall be agreed upon from time to time in writing by the Fund and the Remarketing Agent, times $[●] multiplied by (ii) the actual number of days from and including such first calendar day of the preceding calendar month (or the date hereof in the case of the first such payment) to and including the last calendar day of such preceding calendar month or, if applicable, the date of termination of this Agreement, if earlier, or the date of any prior redemption or liquidation for such share (as the case may be), divided by (b) 360. The Fund reserves the right to unilaterally adjust the rate of compensation with 30 days' notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text implies a rate of compensation agreed upon in writing by both the Fund and the Remarketing Agent. The changed text adds a statement that 'The Fund reserves the right to unilaterally adjust the rate of compensation with 30 days' notice.' This directly contradicts the initial implication of a mutually agreed-upon rate, creating an in-text contradiction as it allows the Fund to alter the agreed-upon rate unilaterally, which would be a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Remarketing Agent shall incur no liability to the Fund, the Investment Adviser, the Calculation and Paying Agent or to any Beneficial Owner (or its Agent Member) or any Holder of the VRRM-MFP Shares in its individual capacity or as Remarketing Agent for any action or failure to act, in connection with its duties under this Agreement and the Supplement or otherwise, except as a result of bad faith, gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part.
perturbation.changed_text: The Remarketing Agent shall incur no liability to the Fund, the Investment Adviser, the Calculation and Paying Agent or to any Beneficial Owner (or its Agent Member) or any Holder of the VRRM-MFP Shares in its individual capacity or as Remarketing Agent for any action or failure to act, in connection with its duties under this Agreement and the Supplement or otherwise, except as a result of bad faith, gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part. However, the Remarketing Agent will be liable for any minor errors in calculations, regardless of intent.
perturbation.explanation: The initial part of the clause provides a broad exception to liability, only for bad faith, gross negligence, or willful misconduct. The addition of 'However, the Remarketing Agent will be liable for any minor errors in calculations, regardless of intent' directly contradicts this. It creates an in-text contradiction because the initial statement broadly protects the Remarketing Agent from liability except in specific serious cases, while the added statement makes them liable for even minor errors, regardless of their intent or severity. This narrows the scope of protection for the remarketing agent, creating an in-text contradiction in Section 11. Remarketing Agent's Performance; Duty of Care
perturbation.contradicted_text: The duties and obligations of the Remarketing Agent shall be determined solely by the express provisions of this Agreement and the Supplement. No implied covenants or obligations shall be read into this Agreement, or the Supplement. In the absence of bad faith on the part of the Remarketing Agent, the Remarketing Agent may conclusively rely upon any document furnished to it, which purports to conform to the requirements of this Agreement and the Statement, as to the truth of the statements expressed in any of such documents. The Remarketing Agent shall be protected in acting upon any document or communication reasonably believed by it to have been signed, presented or made by the proper party or parties. The Remarketing Agent shall incur no liability to the Fund, the Investment Adviser, the Calculation and Paying Agent or to any Beneficial Owner (or its Agent Member) or any Holder of the VRRM-MFP Shares in its individual capacity or as Remarketing Agent for any action or failure to act, in connection with its duties under this Agreement and the Supplement or otherwise, except as a result of bad faith, gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part. However, the Remarketing Agent will be liable for any minor errors in calculations, regardless of intent.
perturbation.justification: The original text exempts the Remarketing Agent from liability except in cases of bad faith, gross negligence, or willful misconduct. The changed text adds an exception: liability for minor errors in calculations, regardless of intent. This directly contradicts the broad liability exemption initially granted, creating an in-text contradiction with the earlier statement within the same section that the Remarketing Agent incurs no liability except for bad faith, gross negligence or willful misconduct. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
